sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 9

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 9:1
items.html: (to the question) The Rucha Kadma'a [first spirit] is imparted from ZA [Zeir Anpin] to the Nukva [female] while both are still incorporated in AVI [Aba ve Ima] — ZA in Aba and the Nukva in Ima — and accordingly they are considered to be in a Zivug Panim be Panim [face-to-face coupling] like AVI themselves. Yet since the Lights of the Zivug are not yet clothed in their own Kelim [vessels], they are for this reason considered, from their own perspective, to be in the state of Achor be Achor [back-to-back]; for those Lights clothe not in them but only in the Kelim of AVI, only with the incorporation of the MAN [Mayin Nukvin] of ZON. (Items 104 and 105)
